cards.title: Our sensitive teen daughter's self-worth tested by social media
cards.body: Advice columnist Eleanor Gordon-Smith addresses how to support a sensitive teen navigating peer pressure and social media challenges. She emphasizes that with time, your daughter's world will expand and she'll find her people.

cards.title: The Problem With Forced Apologies
cards.body: Forced apologies are a common strategy for managing challenging child behavior, but experts question their effectiveness. A better approach may help children develop genuine accountability and emotional understanding.

cards.title: When Your Teen Resists Therapy, Try Curiosity
cards.body: Getting resistant teens into therapy requires a different approach than logical arguments. Dialectical Behavior Therapy techniques like curiosity can help reluctant adolescents engage with mental health support.

cards.title: 4 Core Components of Resilience
cards.body: Resilience isn't a fixed trait—it's made up of four learnable components that anyone can develop. Working on these core elements can help children and adults boost resilience over time.

cards.title: Can ADHD Medication Make Autism Traits More Noticeable?
cards.body: ADHD symptoms can mask autism traits, making them harder to identify in children. Treatment may shift this balance, revealing previously hidden autistic characteristics.

cards.title: Should Teens Share Neurodivergence in College Essays?
cards.body: A former college admissions consultant weighs the pros and cons of disclosing neurodivergence in applications. Strategic disclosure can help admissions teams understand your teen's unique perspective and needs.

cards.title: Why Adults Need to Play
cards.body: Play isn't just for children—research shows adults benefit from it too with cognitive, physical, and social gains. Encouraging playfulness in families can improve wellbeing across generations.
